title: Control Memory Management Policies on a Node — Placing a Pod in the Guaranteed QoS class
text:
If the selected policy is anything other than
None
, the Memory Manager identifies pods
that are in the
Guaranteed
QoS class.
The Memory Manager provides specific topology hints to the Topology Manager for each
Guaranteed
pod.
For pods in a QoS class other than
Guaranteed
, the Memory Manager provides default topology hints
to the Topology Manager.
The following excerpts from pod manifests assign a pod to the
Guaranteed
QoS class.
A Pod with integer CPU(s) runs in the
Guaranteed
QoS class, when
requests
are equal to
limits
:
spec
:
containers
:
-
name
:
nginx
image
:
nginx
resources
:
limits
:
memory
:
"200Mi"
cpu
:
"2"
example.com/device
:
"1"
requests
:
memory
:
"200Mi"
cpu
:
"2"
example.com/device
:
"1"
Also, a pod sharing CPU(s) runs in the
Guaranteed
QoS class, when
requests
are equal to
limits
.
spec
:
containers
:
-
name
:
nginx
image
:
nginx
resources
:
limits
:
memory
:
"200Mi"
cpu
:
"300m"
example.com/device
:
"1"
requests
:
memory
:
"200Mi"
cpu
:
"300m"
example.com/device
:
"1"
Notice that both CPU and memory requests must be specified for a Pod to lend it to Guaranteed QoS class.
